event_name: The Georgia Room Presents: Revolutionary Soldiers of Marietta National Cemetery
blurb: Coming off the Fourth of July weekend, this one hits differently. Marietta National Cemetery historian Brad Quinlin is giving a free presentation at Switzer Library on Monday evening about two American Revolutionary War veterans actually buried right here in Marietta. Their names are John Hames and John Clark, and the story of how soldiers from the Revolution ended up laid to rest in Cobb County is genuinely fascinating. It's an hour of your evening, it's free, and it's the kind of local history most people drive past every day without ever knowing. If you've got a teen who just did a history unit on the Revolution, bring them. If you're the person who always says you want to learn more about the area you live in, here's your chance. Monday, 6 PM, no ticket needed.
scoring_notes: East Cobb skews educated and curious. A specialized local history presentation timed right after Independence Day is a strong fit. Free, local, and genuinely unique content about Marietta's own Revolutionary War connection. Works well for the established family and empty nester crowd.
